calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-13-2011 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Trustee. Eric Bailey and Erin Melcolm (Beneficiaries).

This matter was pregranted.

Mr. Bailey withdrew his objections after speaking with Mr. Siracusa. Counsel will not charge for additional time. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/Ella Yip Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2061-2063 Hayes Street, San Francisco, 94117)
Off Calendar May-13-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Wanda Hutchinson. Phyllis Oscar for and with Eric Bailey and for and with Erin Macolm (Beneficiaries).

Ms. Oscar stated that they had no objection to the sale going forward. The sale was confirmed at $950,000.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Brown.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2061-2063 Hayes Street, San Francisco, 94117)
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Continued To May-09-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Wanda Hutchinson (Trustee). Phyllis Oscar for and with Eric Bailey and Erin Malcolm.

Ms. Oscar stated that she was appearing for the limited purpose of requesting a continuance of the confirmation of sale. The Court stated that it received ex parte communication from Mr. Bailey and Ms. Malcolm indicating that they have learned that there is an insurance policy in an amount undetermined and that it is their desire to ascertain the amount and if sufficient they would use the proceeds to pay off the RAM (reverse mortgage). Ms. Oscar stated that there were two insurance poilicies (Cal Pers and Anthem Life), provided a copy of a letter from Anthem and commitment letter from a Lender. Mr. Siracusa explained status of the reverse mortgage, stated that they received a letter of foreclosure and requested the Court proceed with the sale today. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property was continued to May 9, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to obtain information on the insurance policies and a firm commitment from the Lender. The Court stated that unless the total amount of the policies match the amount to pay off the RAM, the outstanding debts and fees, that there is no reason why the matter cannot go forward.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Authorization To List And Sell Trust'S Real Property And To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Broker
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Trustee.

Mr. Siracusa stated that he e-mailed information to all Parties regarding what was owed, the difficulties they would have as outlined in his declaration, that he received a thank you from a Grandaughter but that he did not hear anything else. The Court granted the petition (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Authorization To List And Sell Trust'S Real Property And To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Broker
Off Calendar Oct-06-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Wanda Hutchinson. Eric Bailey (Grandson) in propria persona. Erin Malcolm (Granddaughter) in propria persona.

Mr. Siracusa stated that there is currently $950,000.00 owed on the loan, that the attendant care bill hasn't been paid, that his client is sympathetic to the grandchildren wanting to keep/buy the property, and that there is another issue of the interest accumulating at $5,000.00 a month. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that he knows that the grandchildren want to get a loan, that he doesn't think that will occur, that the loan can't be at seventy-five percent of the value, that the grandchildren aren't the only beneficiaries, that the sale needs to go forward, and that he has concerns with any delays. Mr. Bailey stated that Financial Freedom will grant an extension, that his Sister is willing to move into his unit to allow the other unit to be rented, that he is attempting to obtain the information that he needs but his access is limited because he doesn't have authority, and that he would like to exhaust all resources in an attempt to keep the property. Erin Malcolm stated that they have tried to contact Ms. Hutchinson but that she hasn't returned their calls. Mr. Siracusa stated that he would provide Mr. Bailey with the current statement from Financial Freedom, stated that he is concerned about additional continuances, and that if the matter is continued out further that the property is being put at risk. The Court continued the matter to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

Petition For Authorization To List And Sell Trust'S Real Property And To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Real Estate Broker
